text.content:
Header Cell at 18pt
This is the main heading for the section with adequate font size for readability

Medium Cell at 10pt
This cell uses a moderate font size that should be readable on most screens but is getting close to the minimum

Small Cell at 8pt
This cell uses a very small font size below the 9pt minimum threshold which should trigger a warning in the quality metrics

Dense text at 8pt with multiple lines
Line 2: Additional context about the metric performance over time
Line 3: Year-over-year comparison shows improvement
Line 4: Target achievement at 95%

Normal text at 12pt
This is a comfortable reading size for presentation content displayed on screen

Tiny text at 7pt
This is way too small for any practical presentation use and should definitely be flagged by quality checks

Large narrative block at 10pt with extensive content
This paragraph demonstrates what happens when you try to fit too much textual content into a shape grid cell. The content should wrap multiple times and potentially overflow the allocated vertical space, which is exactly the kind of issue the eval harness needs to detect and flag.

Acceptable at 9pt
Right at the threshold. Should not trigger font size warnings but may trigger overflow if the content is too long for the cell dimensions.

Mixed sizes would go here
In practice, cells can't mix sizes in shape_grid
But this tests the boundary at 11pt
